title: 10 best herbal decoctions for runny nose
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for runny nose are a natural remedy that combines herbs, spices, and botanicals to create a soothing drink that helps alleviate congestion and sinus pressure. These decoctions work by warming and opening up airways, reducing inflammation, and loosening mucus, providing quick relief from a runny nose. For example, a ginger and eucalyptus decoction can help ease sinus pressure, while a peppermint and lemon balm mixture can calm the throat and nasal passages. By enjoying these herbal concoctions, individuals can breathe easier and sleep better, ultimately improving their daily lives by reducing fatigue and increasing overall comfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with runny nose because of its natural antihistamine properties. The decoction's active compounds, such as artemisinin and isothiocyanates, help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, providing quick relief from excessive mucus production. Additionally, the decoction's anti-inflammatory effects soothe and calm irritated tissues, further reducing nasal discharge and promoting a healthier sinus environment, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating runny nose symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine effects: Purple coneflower contains compounds like quercetin, which has antihistamine properties that can help reduce nasal congestion and runny nose by blocking the action of histamine on nasal tissues.

Anti-inflammatory effects: The decoction's flavonoids and terpenoids have anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating symptoms like congestion and runny nose.

Mucolytic activity: Purple coneflower contains mucolytics like acetylene compounds, which can break down mucus and reduce its thickness, making it easier to expel from the nasal passages and potentially relieving congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower are used to make a decoction that helps to reduce nasal congestion and relieve symptoms of runny nose.
Barks: The barks of the purple coneflower are used to create a decoction that is anti-inflammatory, which can help to alleviate sinus pressure and ease the flow of mucus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated and before midday sun exposure.
Clean and chop one ounce of echinacea purpurea flowers into small pieces ensuring no stems or leaves are present.
Combine the chopped echinacea flowers with four cups of boiling water in a large glass container allowing it to steep for 20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass measuring cup discarding the solids and retaining the liquid.
Store the resulting clear herbal decoction in an airtight glass bottle refrigerated at 39°f to 41°f for up to three days for consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with runny nose because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, particularly glycyrrhizin and flavonoids. These compounds have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing congestion and runniness. Additionally, licorice decoctions can help to reduce mucus production, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving symptoms of a runny nose, such as dripping and sneezing. By promoting a healthy balance in the nasal mucosa, licorice decoctions can provide long-lasting relief from runny nose discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and ease congestion.

Mucilage content: Licorice root contains a type of fiber called mucilage, which forms a soothing gel-like substance when mixed with water. This helps to coat and protect the mucous membranes in the nose, throat, and lungs, providing relief from irritation and runny nose symptoms.

Antihistaminic properties: Licorice root has been shown to have antihistamine effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergies such as runny nose, sneezing, and congestion by blocking the action of histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant are rich in glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate runny nose symptoms.

Barks: The barks of the licorice plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antiviral and antibacterial properties, making them effective against common cold viruses and bacteria that cause runny noses.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and place it in a clean glass container.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the herb, cover the container, and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids.
Add one tablespoon of honey to the decoction and stir well to combine.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion decoctions helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The decoction's bioactive compounds, such as taraxasterol and sesquiterpene lactones, work to reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, thereby alleviating congestion and rhinorrhea (runny nose). Additionally, dandelion's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the airways, promoting a healthy respiratory system. By addressing these underlying issues, dandelion decoctions provide natural relief for runny noses and sinus pressure.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol, help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, relieving sinus pressure and clearing excess mucus.

Antioxidant: Dandelion's rich antioxidant content helps neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate allergies and respiratory issues, reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the nasal tissues.

Expectorant: The mucilages present in dandelion decoctions help loosen and clear thick phlegm from the airways, making it easier to expel excess mucus and congestion, providing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure.
Seeds: The seeds contain a unique combination of fatty acids and polyunsaturated compounds that can help dry up excess mucus and soothe irritated nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2 teaspoons of dried taraxacum officinale herb and transfer it to a heat-resistant container.
Boil one cup of distilled water in a saucepan and pour it over the herb, steeping for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container, discarding the solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.
Consume one quarter cup of the cooled decoction three times daily as needed for relief from runny nose symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Leafy spurge decoctions helps with runny nose because it contains bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The decoction's active ingredients, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, help to reduce nasal congestion by suppressing the release of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Additionally, leafy spurge decoctions may also have a decongestant effect on the nasal passages, reducing excess mucus production and providing relief from runny nose symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Leafy spurge decoctions contain compounds like sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, which reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate congestion and discharge.
Antihistaminic properties: The decoction's phenolic acids and alkaloids can help counteract histamine release in the body, reducing excessive mucus production and relieving symptoms of a runny nose.
Expectorant properties: The decoction's mucilages and saponins can help loosen and clear out excess mucus from the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bitter compounds, roots help to reduce mucus secretion and relieve nasal congestion.
Seeds: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, seeds alleviate sinus pressure and ease nasal discharge.
remedy_recipe: Gather one part dried euphorbia esula roots and rhizomes, weighing approximately 15 grams for a single serving.
Grind the gathered euphorbia esula material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 5 minutes.
Combine the ground euphorbia esula with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container, allowing it to steep for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the resulting decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding any solids and reserving the liquid.
Allow the filtered decoction to cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it in an amber glass bottle for up to three days for consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow decoctions helps with runny nose because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that work synergistically to reduce nasal congestion and sinus pressure. The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties help to alleviate swollen nasal membranes and ease excess mucus production, allowing the body to naturally clear out irritants and allergens more efficiently. As a result, yarrow decoctions can provide fast and effective relief for runny noses caused by colds, allergies, or environmental factors.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as achillein and sesquiterpene lactones, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, relieving congestion and sinus pressure.

Antibacterial properties: Yarrow contains compounds like thymol and carvacrol that exhibit antibacterial activity, which can help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to a runny nose and sinus issues.
Expectorant properties: Yarrow's decoction helps loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to expel phlegm and reducing the amount of congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are valued for their ability to reduce inflammation and congestion, making them effective in soothing a runny nose.
Flowers: Yarrow flowers contain compounds that help to dry up excess mucus, providing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried achillea millefolium herb in quantities of one teaspoon per cup of water.
Combine the dried herb with boiling water and let it steep for ten minutes to release active compounds.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and sediment.
Measure out three cups of the decoction and reduce heat to low for twenty minutes to concentrate essence.
Cool the decoction to room temperature before straining it again and transferring to a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with runny nose because they contain antihistamine and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce nasal congestion and alleviate sinus pressure. The warm, soothing liquid also helps to loosen and clear out mucus and debris from the nasal passages, providing relief from constant sniffing and sneezing. Additionally, elder's natural antibacterial properties can help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the runny nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating congestion and discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in elder decoction, including polyphenols and saponins, inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that cause infections, which can contribute to a runny nose.

Mucolytic properties: Elder decoction's mucolytic compounds, such as mucilage and saponins, help break down mucus in the nasal passages and sinuses, making it easier to expel excess mucus and reduce congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have been traditionally used to soothe and calm congestion, making them a great addition to decoctions for runny noses.
Barks: The barks of the elder plant contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation and ease sinus pressure, making it an effective ingredient in medicinal decoctions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 ounce of dried sambucus nigra flowers from a reputable source.
Combine the harvested flowers with 4 cups of purified water in a large saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days or freeze for later use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold decoctions helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling in the nasal passages. The decoction's expectorant properties also help to loosen and clear out mucus, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure. Additionally, pot marigold contains antioxidants that help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation, further alleviating symptoms associated with a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold decoctions contain flavonoids and terpenes that reduce inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, which helps to alleviate congestion and rhinorrhea (excessive discharge).

Antihistamine and anti-allergic properties: The decoction's flavonoids, particularly luteolin, exhibit antihistamine-like effects, helping to counteract histamine-induced symptoms such as runny nose, sneezing, and itchy eyes.

Mucolytic and expectorant properties: Pot marigold contains saponins that help break down thick mucus and promote its elimination from the body, making it easier to expel and reducing nasal congestion and discharge.
remedy_parts: Roots: Potent anti-inflammatory properties in roots help reduce nasal congestion and relieve runny nose symptoms.
Barks: Tannins present in barks have astringent properties that constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive mucus production and alleviating runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one cup of fresh or dried calendula officinalis flowers or one ounce of dried material.
Combine the harvested calendula with two quarts of boiling water in a large pot.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20-30 minutes to extract the bioactive compounds from the herb.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a clean container, discarding solids.
Allow the cooled decoction to sit for at least four hours before drinking one cup three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena decoctions helps with runny nose because its antiviral properties help to combat the underlying causes of nasal congestion. The decoction's soothing and anti-inflammatory compounds calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing excess mucus production and thinning out stubborn congestion. Additionally, lemon verbena's natural decongestant properties help to ease sinus pressure and clear airways, providing swift relief from runny nose and sinus congestion symptoms. This natural remedy promotes a sense of clarity and comfort, making it an effective solution for soothing a stuffy or runny nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon verbena's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as citral and geraniol, help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, which can ease congestion and sinus pressure.

Decongestant and expectorant properties: The decoction's natural decongestants and expectorants, like citronellal and limonene, thin mucus and promote its expulsion from the body, making it easier to breathe through a runny nose.

Antiviral and antimicrobial properties: Lemon verbena's antiviral and antimicrobial compounds, such as limonene and geraniol, can help combat infections that cause runny nose, reducing the severity of symptoms and promoting recovery.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon verbena have been traditionally used to make a soothing and anti-inflammatory decoction that helps to alleviate congestion and ease respiratory issues, making it effective in treating a runny nose.
Seeds: Lemon verbena seeds are used to create a medicinal decoction that is said to help reduce excessive mucus production and alleviate sinus pressure, providing relief from a runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Collect fresh or dried verbena officinalis leaves and flowers in an airtight container for future use measuring 10 grams of plant material.
Grind the collected verbena officinalis into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle taking approximately 5 minutes.
Combine 250 milliliters of boiling water with 10 grams of ground verbena officinalis powder in a heat-resistant glass bowl waiting for 20-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.
Allow the decoction to cool before drinking it within 2 hours of preparation as a warm tea serving 1 cup twice daily.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum decoctions helps with runny nose because it contains natural expectorants that effectively loosen and clear out excess mucus from the nasal passages. The decongestant properties of eucalyptus oil, a key component of Tasmanian blue gum, help to reduce swelling in the nasal tissues, allowing for easier breathing and a significant reduction in nasal discharge. Additionally, the antiviral and antibacterial properties of the decoction help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the runny nose, providing relief from congestion and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Expectorant and mucolytic properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum decoctions can help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, reducing congestion and runniness. This is due to the presence of volatile oils like eucalyptol and cineole, which thin and break up mucus, making it easier to expel.

Anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties: Decoctions of Tasmanian Blue Gum may reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating discomfort and reducing the production of excess mucous. This is attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.

Decongestant properties: The menthol content in Tasmanian Blue Gum decoctions can help constrict blood vessels, reducing swelling in the nasal tissues and alleviating congestion. This decongestant effect may also contribute to the reduction of runny nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce nasal discharge and alleviate congestion.
Barks: Containing tannins and flavonoids, which aid in decongesting the nasal passages and soothing irritated mucous membranes.
remedy_recipe: Rinse one teaspoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in cold water for 30 seconds to remove impurities.
Boil one cup of fresh filtered water in a medium-sized saucepan for five minutes at a rolling boil.
Add the rinsed eucalyptus globulus leaves to the boiling water and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid reaches 100°f.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve into a heatproof cup or mug, discarding the solids after 5 minutes.
Allow the decoction to cool for five minutes before consuming one quarter cup up to three times daily as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenrod decoctions helps with runny nose because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce congestion and ease sinus pressure. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in goldenrod have been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are known to cause blood vessels to swell, leading to increased mucus secretion. By reducing inflammation and swelling in the nasal passages, goldenrod decoctions can help to alleviate runny nose symptoms and promote a sense of relief for those suffering from allergies or respiratory issues.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine-like compounds: Goldenrod contains flavonoids and terpenes that have antihistamine-like effects, helping to reduce nasal congestion and itching by counteracting histamine's effects on blood vessels.

Anti-inflammatory agents: The decoction's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce swelling in the nasal passages, sinuses, and eyes, which alleviates discomfort and promotes drainage of excess mucus.
Mucolytic activity: Goldenrod contains mucolytic compounds that break down thick or excess mucus, making it easier to expel and relieving congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bitter compounds, roots help to dry up excess mucus and reduce nasal discharge.
Rhyzomes: Containing anti-inflammatory properties, rhyzomes soothe and calm irritated nasal tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest solidago virgaurea leaves and stems, drying them thoroughly to preserve potency and aroma.
Combine 20-30 grams of dried solidago virgaurea with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass container.
Simmer the mixture for 15-20 minutes, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove solids and preserve the medicinal properties.
Consume the final product within 24 hours of preparation, dosing at 1 cup every 3-4 hours as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating a runny nose. Additionally, chamomile's soothing effects can calm irritated mucous membranes, reducing excessive mucus production and promoting healthy drainage. As a result, drinking chamomile decoctions can provide quick relief from congestion and sinus pressure associated with a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, relieving discomfort and allowing for easier breathing.

Antihistamine-like action: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenes have antihistamine-like effects, which help to reduce histamine release and alleviate symptoms such as runny nose, sneezing, and itchy eyes.

Sothing and calming: Chamomile's soothing and calming properties can help to reduce nasal secretions and calm the mucous membranes, providing relief from excessive mucus production and associated discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce nasal congestion and relieve sinus pressure.
Flowers: The flowers are used for their soothing and calming effects on the mucous membranes in the nose, reducing inflammation and irritation.
remedy_recipe: Gather one teaspoon of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers for every eight ounces of water.
Boil water in a pot and then reduce heat to simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers to the simmering water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before drinking one to two cups per day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal decoctions helps with runny nose because it contains berberine, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. When taken orally or inhaled as a steam inhalation, goldenseal can help reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from a runny nose. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of goldenseal may also help to combat free radicals that can exacerbate cold and sinus symptoms. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, goldenseal decoctions can provide effective natural relief for a runny nose.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Berberine, a bioactive alkaloid found in goldenseal, has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce nasal congestion and alleviate symptoms of a runny nose by inhibiting the growth of bacteria and fungi that may contribute to sinus infections.

Isoquinoline alkaloids: Isoquinoline alkaloids present in goldenseal decoctions have antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, which can help combat viral infections that cause runny nose and congestion, while also boosting the body's natural defense mechanisms.

Beta-carotene: Goldenseal contains beta-carotene, a precursor to vitamin A that has antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm irritated mucous membranes in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy sinus function.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in berberine, a natural antihistamine and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce congestion and alleviate symptoms of runny nose.
Rhyzomes: Contains berberine and other compounds that help to soothe the nasal passages and reduce inflammation, making it effective for treating runny nose.
remedy_recipe: Measure one teaspoon of dried goldenseal root powder into a small bowl.
Combine the measured goldenseal root powder with two cups of boiling water in a medium saucepan.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes to allow active compounds to release and mingle.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass container.
Store the cooled decoction in the refrigerator for up to three days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail decoctions helps with runny nose because of its natural antihistamine properties, which effectively combat excess mucus production. The decoction's expectorant qualities help loosen and clear out congestion, reducing nasal secretions and providing relief from a stuffy or runny nose. remedy_recipe: Harvest equisetum arvense roots in early spring or fall, ensuring freshness and quality for optimal efficacy.
Rinse the harvested roots under cold running water, removing any dirt or debris prior to processing.
Dry the rinsed roots in a warm, dark place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content for decoction preparation.
Combine one tablespoon of dried equisetum arvense root with one cup of boiling water, steeping for 15-20 minutes to extract beneficial compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve, discarding solids and transferring the liquid to a clean container for consumption.

Seeds: Contains anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa petals and leaves for their medicinal properties.
Combine one teaspoon of dried rosa rugosa flowers with boiling water in a cup.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to release.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle.
Store the herbal decoction in the refrigerator and consume within 24 hours.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried symphytum officinale herb measuring one teaspoon per cup of water.
Combine the herb with boiling water in a saucepan and steep for five minutes.
Strain the mixture using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth into a large bowl.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature within thirty minutes.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to twenty-four hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with runny nose because of its natural expectorant properties. remedy_recipe: Weigh 20-30 grams of dried foeniculum vulgare and place it in a heat-resistant container.
Combine the herb with 500 milliliters of boiling water, then reduce heat to low for 10-15 minutes.
Allow the mixture to steep for 45-60 minutes, occasionally stirring, before straining it through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve.
Discard the solids and transfer the liquid decoction to a clean glass container, storing it in the refrigerator at 2-8 degrees celsius.
Use the prepared decoction within 24 hours, dosing as directed by a healthcare professional, typically 1/2 teaspoon thrice daily with food.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender decoctions helps with runny nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The decoction's antiviral and antibacterial compounds help to combat underlying infections that can cause excessive mucus production, reducing congestion and discomfort. remedy_recipe: Harvest one ounce of fresh or dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and store them in an airtight container for later use.
Boil one quart of water in a pot and then reduce heat to simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Add one teaspoon of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers to the simmering water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding solids.
Allow the decoction to cool to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage decoctions helps with runny nose because of its natural ability to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages. remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried salvia officinalis leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Measure out 200 milliliters of boiling water and carefully pour it over the ground herb in a heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container.
Discard the solids and transfer the decoction to a clean glass bottle, labeling it with the date and time of preparation.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40 degrees fahrenheit for up to 3 days before consuming it as needed.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat runny nose with herbal decoctions is to combine ingredients that have natural anti-inflammatory, decongestant, and expectorant properties. Mix equal parts of dried Echinacea, Goldenseal, and Slippery Elm in boiling water, then strain and drink as a tea three times a day. This combination helps reduce nasal congestion, soothes the throat, and promotes healthy mucus clearance.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to runny nose that are treated with herbal decoctions are sinusitis, bronchitis, and congestion. Herbal decoctions containing ingredients like eucalyptus, ginger, and turmeric help relieve respiratory issues by reducing inflammation, opening airways, and loosening mucus. Other ailments that benefit from herbal decoctions include coughs, colds, and flu, as well as allergies and asthma.
